headline: Dollar Tree entered into a new $500 million term loan credit facility maturing in 2029 and terminated its existing 364-day revolving credit agreement.
investor_impact: This new $500 million term loan facility secures medium-term liquidity for Dollar Tree at a reasonable spread of SOFR plus 1.00%, replacing its expiring 364-day revolver. The lack of required amortization and flexible prepayment terms provide the company with improved financial flexibility and a stable capital structure through 2029.
key_figures: Term SOFR Rate plus 1.00% initial interest rate
March 20, 2026 expiration of existing 364-day revolving credit agreement
Bank of America, N.A. as agent
